title: 7 Expected Dividend Increases In September
text: McDonald's, Starbucks, Lockheed Martin, Microsoft, Philip Morris, Texas Instruments, and Verizon are expected to announce dividend increases in September 2023. McDonald's and Starbucks have strong dividend growth histories and are expected to announce solid increases. Lockheed Martin, Microsoft, Philip Morris, Texas Instruments, and Verizon are expected to announce modest dividend increases.

title: Nordson TEST & INSPECTION Showcases State-of-the-Art Inspection and Metrology Solutions at SEMICON Taiwan
text: MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Nordson TEST & INSPECTION today announced its participation in SEMICON Taiwan, one of the most anticipated events in the semiconductor industry. The company will be showcasing its state-of-the-art inspection and metrology solutions, including the CyberOptics SQ3000™+ Multi-Function system, the new Quadra 7 Pro Manual X-ray Inspection (MXI) System, and the revolutionary WaferSense® Auto Teaching System™ (ATS2). The event will take place from Sept. 6-8, 2023 at T.

title: Nordson (NDSN) Closes ARAG Deal, Boosts Industrial Precision Unit

title: Nordson (NDSN) Beats on Q3 Earnings, Narrows 2023 Guidance
text: Nordson's (NDSN) third-quarter fiscal 2023 results reflect a year-over-year decline in the top line due to ongoing pressure in electronics and biopharma end markets. The company narrows its 2023 outlook.

title: Nordson (NDSN) Surpasses Q3 Earnings Estimates
text: Nordson (NDSN) came out with quarterly earnings of $2.35 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.31 per share. This compares to earnings of $2.49 per share a year ago.

title: Nordson (NDSN) Q3 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
text: Although the revenue and EPS for Nordson (NDSN) give a sense of how its business performed in the quarter ended July 2023, it might be worth considering how some key metrics compare with Wall Street estimates and the year-ago numbers.

title: Nordson stock drops 7% after company's revenue miss
text: Nordson Corp. shares NDSN, -0.41% fell nearly 7% in the aftermarket Monday after the maker of adhesives and other industrial products reported fiscal third-quarter revenue that missed estimates, mostly on “ongoing” weakness in its electronics and biopharma businesses. Nordson earned $128 million, or $2.22 a share, in the quarter, compared with $142 million, or $2.45 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Nordson Corporation Reports Third Quarter Fiscal 2023 Results and Narrows Annual Guidance
text: WESTLAKE, Ohio--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Nordson Corporation (Nasdaq: NDSN) today reported results for the fiscal third quarter ended July 31, 2023. Sales were $649 million, a 2% decrease compared to the prior year's third quarter sales of $662 million. The decrease in third quarter 2023 sales includes an organic decrease of 5%, partially offset by a favorable acquisition impact. The organic sales decrease was driven by ongoing pressure in electronics, primarily semiconductor dispense applications, and.

title: 6 Upcoming Dividend Increases, Including A Dividend King
text: Dividend investors prefer companies with a track record of increasing dividends, indicating strong business performance and shareholder rewards. The article provides lists of stocks expected to increase dividends in the coming week, based on companies with at least five years of consistent dividend growth. title: Nordson Corporation Board of Directors Increases Dividend 5 Percent, Marking 60 Consecutive Years of Annual Dividend Increases
text: WESTLAKE, Ohio--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Nordson Corporation (Nasdaq: NDSN) today announced that its Board of Directors has approved a 5 percent increase in the Company's quarterly cash dividend to $0.68 per common share from $0.65. The dividend is payable on September 5, 2023, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on August 22, 2023. title: The Ultimate 10 Retirement Salvation Dividend Aristocrats For August
text: The answer is simple for those worried about high market valuations hurting future returns. Don't buy overvalued stocks. While the average investor is at risk of a lost decade or two, smart investors buying fast-growing aristocrats trading at reasonable prices have nothing to fear.
